items.headline: Owen Tippett internal bleeding missed second round of playoffs for Philadelphia
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Owen Tippett did not play for the Philadelphia Flyers in the Eastern Conference Second Round because of an internal bleeding issue, the forward said Tuesday.
Tippett sustained the injury during the Flyers' six-game series win against the Pittsburgh Penguins in the first round.

items.headline: Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen to start in goal for Buffalo in Game 4 of Eastern 2nd Round
items.description:
Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen will start in goal for the Buffalo Sabres in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Montreal Canadiens at Bell Centre in Montreal on Tuesday (7 p.m. ET; CBC, TVAS, SN, ESPN).
The goalie has backed up Alex Lyon the past seven games since being

items.headline: Mats Zuccarello still enjoying game with Wild on brink in West second round
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Mats Zuccarello still feels the tingles going through his body when he walks out of the dressing room, down the tunnel and steps onto the ice before every game.
"I feel like when you're nervous, at least for me, that means it actually means something to

items.headline: NHL EDGE stats Mitch Marner breakout Stanley Cup Playoffs 2026
items.description: NHL.com's fantasy staff continues to cover the latest trends and storylines in the League through the lens of NHL EDGE puck and player tracker stats. Today, we identify some key advanced metrics behind Vegas Golden Knights forward Mitch Marner.

items.headline: Guerin MacFarland Verbeek  finalists for 2025 NHL GM of Year Award
items.description:
Bill Guerin of the Minnesota Wild, Chris MacFarland of the Colorado Avalanche and Pat Verbeek of the Anaheim Ducks are the finalists for the Jim Gregory General Manager of the Year Award.
The award has been presented annually since 2009-10 to the general manager who best excelled at his role

items.headline: General Manager of Year Jim Gregory Award finalists for 2025-26 announced
items.description:
NEW YORK -- Bill Guerin of the Minnesota Wild, Chris MacFarland of the Colorado Avalanche and Pat Verbeek of the Anaheim Ducks are the three finalists for the 2025-26 Jim Gregory General Manager of the Year Award, the National Hockey League announced today.
Voting for this award was conducted among

items.headline: Charlie Coyle signs 6 year contract with Columbus Blue Jackets
items.description:
Charlie Coyle signed a six-year, $36 million contract with the Columbus Blue Jackets on Tuesday. It has an average annual value of $6 million.
The 34-year-old center had 58 points (20 goals, 38 assists) in 82 games with the Blue Jackets this season, his first with them after being acquired

items.headline: Colorado bonds over 'Survivor', on cusp of 2026 West Final
items.description:
DENVER -- It's a conversation some of the Colorado Avalanche anxiously wait to have, usually every Thursday.
What did you think of the latest episode of "Survivor"?
"Me and 'O' (forward Logan O'Connor) every Wednesday recording, Thursday talking about it," Avalanche forward Parker Kelly said of the long-running reality show.

items.headline: Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback for May 12, 2026
items.description: The Stanley Cup Playoffs are in full swing and NHL.com has it all covered. Each day, we will present Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback, your one-stop shop for all the action. Here’s a look at where things stand on Tuesday, May 12:

items.headline: Minnesota Wild did not have an answer in crucial Game 4
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- John Hynes summarized what went wrong for the Minnesota Wild fairly succinctly.
“The style of game we needed to play to win the game, we made the conscious choice not to play that way tonight,” the coach said after the Wild lost to the Colorado Avalanche

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche showcase scoring depth in Game 4 win
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- The Colorado Avalanche's response started with a center-ice face-off win by Nazem Kadri in the aftermath of Nico Sturm's game-tying goal midway through the third period.
What followed were three big-boy shifts capped by a goal from Parker Kelly, the game-winner in a 5-2 victory against

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche Minnesota Wild Game 4 recap May 11 2026
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Parker Kelly scored his first career Stanley Cup Playoff goal in the third period, and the Colorado Avalanche defeated the Minnesota Wild 5-2 in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Grand Casino Arena on Monday.​

items.headline: Artturi Lehkonen Sam Malinski out for Colorado Avalanche in Game 4
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Artturi Lehkonen and Sam Malinski did not play for the Colorado Avalanche in a 5-2 win against the Minnesota Wild in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Grand Casino Arena on Monday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.
